action.reasoning: Observation: baseline greedy utility neighbor selection achieved exploration_score=0.0196, success_rate=0.02, explored_rate=0.74 (very low completion rate within 128 steps). Analysis: myopic local choice of max-utility neighbor leads to distraction and inefficient coverage; global selection of best utility/distance target + shortest-path stepping (via BFS on provided node graph) is a standard frontier-exploration improvement that should raise success_rate substantially while keeping travel_distance reasonable. Zero learned params, fully compatible with ExplorationEnv. Decision: rewrite predict.py with robust BFS-based global heuristic (utility/(dist+1)), update train.py for new strategy name, execute train+local test on available maps as proxy, then submit_eval to get full score and measure iteration time.

command_output.stdout: Saved improved heuristic model to checkpoints/baseline
